text_draft:
The spoon broke.
Pink Bear sat on the cold windowsill, holding the two pieces. Below, the snow kept falling. Soft. Relentless.
She had tried. She had really tried.
Susie appeared beside her, quiet. She picked up Pink Bear and held her close.
"It's okay," Susie whispered. But her voice wavered.
Pink Bear felt the weight of it all—the garden still buried, the hunt still impossible, her girl's disappointment pressing against her like the snow itself.
Everything depended on what happened next.
